commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=2a56c75143a9d431b3c5ac9ab47742ca67c8f148)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes the first-assistant session persistence path from appending the full in-memory JSONL transcript to rewriting the session file, and adds a filesystem-backed SessionManager test.

PR surface: Source 0, Tests +68. Total +68 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows the failure path: open a no-assistant transcript, append another non-assistant message so `flushed` becomes false, then append the first assistant and current main appends the full in-memory buffer over existing disk entries.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor-side real behavior proof and a corrected regression test; automation cannot supply the contributor environment proof.

target.title: fix(sessions): rewrite first assistant flush
target.body:
## Summary
- Fixes the first-assistant flush path so a resumed no-assistant transcript is rewritten to the current in-memory buffer instead of appending that whole buffer onto existing disk contents.
- This prevents duplicate session headers and duplicate pending user turns when resuming `header + user-only` transcripts.
- Adds focused filesystem-backed coverage for the resume path.

## Linked context
Closes #89206.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)
- Behavior or issue addressed: resumed session persistence duplicated existing transcript entries when the first assistant message caused the unflushed buffer to be written.
- Real environment tested: disposable temp JSONL session file through `SessionManager.open(...)` and `appendMessage(...)`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `PNPM_CONFIG_OFFLINE=true corepack pnpm test src/agents/sessions/session-manager.test.ts -- --reporter=dot`.
- Evidence after fix: the regression passes and asserts the on-disk transcript has exactly `session`, `message`, `message`, with one preserved user message.
- Observed result after fix: first assistant flush rewrites the session file once instead of appending the full buffer over existing entries.
- What was not tested: no live production resume was run against a real user transcript.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: I used a disposable temp transcript so private/local session data is not touched.

## Tests and validation
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/agents/sessions/session-manager.ts src/agents/sessions/session-manager.test.ts`
- `PNPM_CONFIG_OFFLINE=true corepack pnpm test src/agents/sessions/session-manager.test.ts -- --reporter=dot` passed 1 Vitest file / 1 test.
- `git diff --check -- src/agents/sessions/session-manager.ts src/agents/sessions/session-manager.test.ts`

## Risk checklist
- Did user-visible behavior change? Yes, resumed transcripts should no longer gain duplicate entries on first assistant flush.
- Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? No.
- Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No.
- Highest-risk area: session persistence semantics. Mitigated by keeping the change to the existing unflushed first-assistant branch and covering the reported resume shape with a real temp file.

## Current review state
Ready for maintainer review. No bot/reviewer comments have been addressed yet.
target.author: charles-openclaw

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
